name: correctness
focus: semantic correctness, realistic edge cases, test adequacy, regressions, and parity with nearby code patterns where those patterns affect correctness
constraints: Do not ask for redesigns unless required for correctness.
Treat pre-existing limitations as out of scope unless the patch makes them worse.

name: maintainability
focus: local consistency, future extension cost, duplication drift risk, naming, comments, and fit with the repository's existing patterns
constraints: Prefer local consistency over idealized design.
Do not push broad refactors unless the patch creates a real maintenance risk.

name: scope-and-regression
focus: whether the patch is narrowly scoped, avoids unintended behavior changes, and does not introduce unnecessary coupling
constraints: Do not ask for unrelated improvements.

name: edge-cases
focus: boundary conditions, empty and singleton inputs, configuration or environment edge cases, state transitions, and happy-path-only tests
constraints: Focus on realistic edge cases for the specific patch.
Do not ask for speculative hardening unless there is concrete risk.
